ucs.title: [LLM] Qilin (Agenda) GPO logon-script Chrome credential harvest
ucs.description: Hunts Qilin's signature pre-encryption credential sweep, in which the affiliate pushes a PowerShell logon-script GPO (publicly seen as 'IPScanner.ps1' / 'logins.ps1') that copies Chrome's 'Login Data' SQLite DB and 'Local State' from every interactive user across the domain. Tied to Qilin specifically by Sophos' July/Aug-2024 incident reporting — distinct from typical ransomware infostealer behaviour because the harvester runs out of gpscript.exe/userinit.exe at logon, on every workstation, fanning out via Group Policy.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.user) as user values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("gpscript.exe","userinit.exe","explorer.exe","svchost.exe") AND Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","powershell_ise.exe") AND (Processes.process="*Login Data*" OR Processes.process="*Local State*" OR Processes.process="*\\Google\\Chrome\\User Data*" OR Processes.process="*\\Microsoft\\Edge\\User Data*" OR Processes.process="*IPScanner*" OR Processes.process="*logins.ps1*" OR Processes.process="*Invoke-WebRequest*tor2web*" OR Processes.process="*-enc *") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name host | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=left dest [| tstats summariesonly=true count as host_hits values(Processes.user) as targeted_users from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name="gpscript.exe" Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe") by Processes.dest | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`] | where host_hits>=3 | sort - lastTime
ucs.kql:
let lookback = 7d;
let susp = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("gpscript.exe","userinit.exe","explorer.exe","svchost.exe")
| where FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","powershell_ise.exe")
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("Login Data","Local State",@"\Google\Chrome\User Data",@"\Microsoft\Edge\User Data","IPScanner","logins.ps1","key4.db","key3.db","signons.sqlite","-enc ","-EncodedCommand","FromBase64String","tor2web","onion")
| extend ChromeArtefact = ProcessCommandLine has_any ("Login Data","Local State",@"\Google\Chrome\User Data",@"\Microsoft\Edge\User Data")
| project Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName, InitiatingProcessFileName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine, ChromeArtefact;
susp
| summarize Hosts=dcount(DeviceId), Users=dcount(AccountName), Sample=any(ProcessCommandLine), FirstSeen=min(Timestamp), LastSeen=max(Timestamp) by InitiatingProcessFileName
| where Hosts >= 3 and Users >= 3
| join kind=inner (susp | where ChromeArtefact) on InitiatingProcessFileName
| project FirstSeen, LastSeen, Hosts, Users, Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, InitiatingProcessFileName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine
| sort by LastSeen desc
ucs.rationale: Generic infostealers touch Chrome 'Login Data' too, but they almost never spawn from gpscript.exe/userinit.exe across many hosts simultaneously. That fan-out pattern is the Qilin tradecraft fingerprint Sophos described in the Synnovis-era cluster — affiliates abusing a domain-wide logon-script GPO to weaponise every workstation as a credential-collection node before staging Agenda's encryptor. Joi

ucs.title: [LLM] Qilin pre-encryption boot-tamper + EDR-kill chain (safeboot + shadow wipe + Defender disable)
ucs.description: Hunts Qilin/Agenda's documented pre-detonation playbook: bcdedit /set safeboot network, reboot into Safe Mode to bypass tamper-protected EDR, then vssadmin/wbadmin shadow wipe and wevtutil log clear before launching the Rust/Go encryptor. The combination of bcdedit safeboot + shadow deletion + Defender exclusion within a short window on the same host is far more specific to Qilin/BlackCat-lineage RaaS than to commodity wipers.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmds values(Processes.process_name) as procs values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (   (Processes.process_name="bcdedit.exe" AND Processes.process="*safeboot*")  OR (Processes.process_name="bcdedit.exe" AND Processes.process="*recoveryenabled*no*")  OR (Processes.process_name="vssadmin.exe" AND (Processes.process="*delete shadows*" OR Processes.process="*resize shadowstorage*"))  OR (Processes.process_name="wmic.exe" AND Processes.process="*shadowcopy*delete*")  OR (Processes.process_name="wbadmin.exe" AND Processes.process="*delete catalog*")  OR (Processes.process_name="wevtutil.exe" AND Processes.process="*cl *")  OR (Processes.process_name="powershell.exe" AND (Processes.process="*Set-MpPreference*DisableRealtimeMonitoring*" OR Processes.process="*Add-MpPreference*ExclusionPath*" OR Processes.process="*Uninstall-WindowsFeature*Windows-Defender*"))  OR (Processes.process_name="net.exe" AND Processes.process="*stop*" AND (Processes.process="*MBAMService*" OR Processes.process="*SentinelAgent*" OR Processes.process="*CSFalcon*" OR Processes.process="*WinDefend*"))  OR (Processes.process_name="shutdown.exe" AND Processes.process="*/r*")) by Processes.dest Processes.user host | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | eval action=case(  match(cmds,"safeboot"),"safeboot",  match(cmds,"shadow|shadowcopy|delete catalog"),"shadow_wipe",  match(cmds,"DisableRealtime|ExclusionPath|Windows-Defender|SentinelAgent|CSFalcon|MBAMService|WinDefend"),"edr_kill",  match(cmds,"wevtutil"),"log_clear",  match(cmds,"shutdown"),"reboot") | stats dc(action) as action_variety values(action) as actions values(cmds) as all_cmds min(firstTime) as firstTime max(lastTime) as lastTime by dest user | where action_variety>=3 AND (lastTime-firstTime) < 3600 | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
let win = 1h;
let evts = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(2d)
| extend cmd = tolower(ProcessCommandLine)
| extend bucket = case(
    FileName =~ "bcdedit.exe" and (cmd has "safeboot" or cmd has "recoveryenabled no"), "safeboot",
    (FileName =~ "vssadmin.exe" and cmd has_any ("delete shadows","resize shadowstorage"))
      or (FileName =~ "wmic.exe" and cmd has "shadowcopy" and cmd has "delete")
      or (FileName =~ "wbadmin.exe" and cmd has "delete catalog"), "shadow_wipe",
    FileName =~ "wevtutil.exe" and cmd has " cl ", "log_clear",
    FileName =~ "powershell.exe" and cmd has_any ("set-mppreference","add-mppreference","disablerealtimemonitoring","exclusionpath","uninstall-windowsfeature"), "defender_off",
    FileName =~ "net.exe" and cmd has "stop" and cmd has_any ("sentinelagent","csfalcon","mbamservice","windefend","sense","sophos"), "edr_stop",
    FileName =~ "shutdown.exe" and cmd has "/r", "reboot",
    "")
| where bucket != "";
evts
| summarize Buckets=make_set(bucket), Cmds=make_set(ProcessCommandLine,50), FirstSeen=min(Timestamp), LastSeen=max(Timestamp), Actor=any(InitiatingProcessAccountName) by DeviceId, DeviceName
| extend Variety = array_length(Buckets), Window = (LastSeen - FirstSeen)
| where Variety >= 3 and Window <= win
| extend Qilin_Score = iff(Buckets has "safeboot" and Buckets has "shadow_wipe", "HIGH", "MEDIUM")
| sort by LastSeen desc
ucs.rationale: Most ransomware crews delete shadow copies, but the bcdedit safeboot pivot to bypass tamper-protected EDR is a hallmark of Qilin/Agenda (and BlackCat-derived affiliates that overlap with Qilin's affiliate pool) reported by Trend Micro and Group-IB. Requiring 3+ distinct buckets within a 1-hour window on a single host filters out admin housekeeping (one-off shadow resize, one-off Defender exclusion
